_ai: AI fills this. Derived from: personas.json + functional-requirements.json + glossary.json. Do not edit manually.

domain.name: BookVault
domain.description: Multi-tenant appointment scheduling platform. Core domain is the appointment lifecycle within strict per-tenant data isolation. Every entity is scoped to a tenant_id enforced by RLS.

entities.name: Tenant
entities.description: A registered business on BookVault. Owns all configuration, staff, services, and appointments within its boundary. Every row in the system is scoped to a tenant_id enforced by RLS.
entities.patternNote: Root of the tenancy model. Status machine governs lifecycle transitions. tenant.provisioned event fans out to billing, notification, and config services on registration.

entities.name: TenantConfig
entities.description: Operational parameters a tenant admin configures: cancellation policy window, forward booking window, max concurrent bookings per customer, and notification preferences.
entities.patternNote: Value object child of Tenant. Mutations produce a tenant.config.updated event for downstream reconfiguration.

entities.name: Appointment
entities.description: The core aggregate. A confirmed reservation between a customer and a staff member for a specific service at a specific time. Current state is derived by replaying the ordered event history — never from a status column.
entities.patternNote: Event Sourcing. Fold over AppointmentEvent log to derive current state. Published via Transactional Outbox to SNS topic for fan-out to all downstream consumers.

entities.name: AppointmentEvent
entities.description: An immutable record of a state transition in an Appointment's lifecycle. The event log is the system's source of truth for all derived views.
entities.patternNote: Append-only. Written in the same DB transaction as the state change, then relayed to SNS via Transactional Outbox. Envelope must satisfy BR013 (tenant_id, event_id, correlation_id, occurred_at).

entities.name: SlotLock
entities.description: An exclusive reservation on a (tenant_id, staff_id, slot_date, slot_start) tuple placed at booking saga start. Prevents concurrent bookings of the same slot. Released on saga compensation or appointment confirmed.
entities.patternNote: Unique DB constraint on (tenant_id, staff_id, slot_date, slot_start) WHERE released_at IS NULL. Core enforcement of BR001. Null released_at = active lock.

entities.name: Staff
entities.description: An employee or contractor whose time is managed on the platform. Child of Tenant. Their working hours and blocks define the availability envelope for the Availability Service.
entities.patternNote: Child entity of Tenant aggregate. Deactivating a staff member does not cancel existing confirmed appointments.

entities.name: WorkingHours
entities.description: The regular weekly schedule for a staff member. Used by the Availability Service as the outer boundary for open slot computation.
entities.patternNote: Value object. Slots outside working hours are never surfaced to customers (BR003).

entities.name: StaffBlock
entities.description: A manual period of unavailability set by a staff member or admin. Intersecting slots are excluded from the availability computation regardless of working hours.
entities.patternNote: Subtracted from working hours in Availability Service slot generation.

entities.name: Service
entities.description: A bookable offering defined by a tenant. Duration drives slot length; price drives payment amount. Child of Tenant.
entities.patternNote: Child entity of Tenant. duration_minutes determines how the Availability Service segments working hours into bookable slots.

entities.name: Customer
entities.description: The person who booked an appointment. Stored as a snapshot on the appointment — not as a global entity — to minimise PII scope and simplify erasure.
entities.patternNote: PII scoped per-tenant. Pseudonymised on GDPR right-to-erasure request. customer_id used for max_advance_bookings enforcement (BR008).

entities.name: Payment
entities.description: A payment or refund record tied to an appointment. Tracks the external provider reference and status independently from the appointment lifecycle.
entities.patternNote: Retained for minimum 7 years per BR012. Refund failure routes to DLQ per BR009 — slot is released regardless.

entities.name: Notification
entities.description: A communication dispatched to a customer or staff member at a lifecycle event. Content is derived entirely from the triggering event payload — no back-calls to booking services.
entities.patternNote: Fully decoupled async consumer of appointment events. Failure never blocks the triggering booking operation (BR007). Retried up to 4 times with exponential backoff before routing to DLQ.

entities.name: DashboardView
entities.description: A pre-aggregated materialized read model of a tenant's daily booking metrics. Updated incrementally per booking event and never queried from the write store.
entities.patternNote: CQRS read model. O(1) reads regardless of booking history depth. Fully rebuildable from AppointmentEvent log via event replay.

entities.name: AuditLog
entities.description: An immutable, append-only record of every write operation that modifies tenant-scoped data. Required for GDPR compliance and incident forensics.
entities.patternNote: INSERT-only PostgreSQL table. booking_app_user is limited to INSERT privilege only. Permanent retention per BR014.
